subtopics.title: Components
subtopics.content:
- **1** double-sided race track board
- **6** race car pawns in different colors
- **44** speed cards (6-8 per car color, each showing movement values for multiple cars)
- **6** car power cards (unique abilities)
- **Betting sheets** (1 per player)
- **Money tokens**

subtopics.title: Board Setup
subtopics.content:
1. **Choose track** — Select side A or B of the board.
2. **Place cars** on the starting line.
3. **Shuffle speed cards** — Deal 8 to each player (varies by player count).
4. **Auction cars** — Players bid for car ownership using speed cards.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
- **Bid for cars** — Each car is auctioned; highest bidder pays and owns that car.
- **Power cards** — Each owned car gives a unique power.
- **Betting sheet** — Track your bets on which cars will win.
- **All players move all cars** — You're not limited to your own car.

subtopics.title: Game Overview
subtopics.content: Downforce is a racing and betting game. Auction for car ownership, play speed cards that move ALL cars, and bet on which cars will win. You profit from owning winning cars and making correct bets.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
On your turn:
1. **Play 1 speed card** — Each card shows movement values for multiple cars.
2. **Move cars** — Move each car listed on the card by its shown value, starting from the top.
3. **Betting checkpoints** — When any car crosses a betting line, all players may place or update bets.

subtopics.title: Key Actions
subtopics.content:
#### Speed Cards
- Each card shows numbers for several car colors.
- Move cars in order from top to bottom of the card.
- Cars cannot pass through blocked spaces; excess movement is lost.

#### Betting
- At each of 3 checkpoints, secretly bet on which car you think will win.
- Earlier correct bets pay more.
- You can bet on any car, not just your own.

#### Car Powers
- Each car has a unique power (e.g., extra movement, ignoring terrain, etc.).
- Only the car's owner can use its power.

subtopics.title: Special Rules
subtopics.content:
- **Blocking** — Cars cannot pass through spaces occupied by other cars in narrow track sections.
- **Wild cards** — Some speed cards have wild values that let you choose which car gets the movement.
- **All cars move** — Every speed card moves multiple cars; you can't avoid helping opponents.

subtopics.title: Scoring and Endgame
subtopics.content:
#### Race End
- Race ends when all cars cross the finish line (or are clearly unable to).

#### Scoring
- **Car ownership payouts** — Prize money based on your car's finishing position.
- **Correct bets** — Payout if your bet was correct (higher for earlier bets).
- **Minus auction cost** — Subtract what you paid for your car(s).
- **Highest net profit wins.**

subtopics.title: Beginner Tips
subtopics.content:
- **Don't overpay in auction** — Money spent on cars reduces your final profit.
- **Bet smart** — Watch track position and cards played to make informed bets.
- **Help your car** — Play cards that maximize your car's movement.
- **Block when possible** — Position cars to block opponents in narrow sections.

subtopics.title: Key Strategies
subtopics.content:
- **Bet diversification** — Spread bets across checkpoints for maximum payout potential.
- **Card counting** — Track which speed cards have been played to predict future movement.
- **Blocking tactics** — Use early movement to position blockers in narrow track sections.
- **Auction value** — Cars with more remaining speed cards in the deck are worth more.
